title: Adaptive 1-dimensional time invariant learning for inertial sensor-based gait authentication

biometric.body_location: multiple (left/right pocket, left/right hand, handbag, backpack)

method.input_representation: 1D Local Ternary Patterns (LTP) with adaptive threshold
method.training_strategy: Unsupervised domain adaptation to align source and target gait distributions across time

evaluation.context: Achieved on MMUISD dataset for slow walking speed and right pocket position using Sigmoid activation
Achieved on MMUISD dataset for slow walking speed and right pocket position using Sigmoid activation
Achieved on MMUISD dataset for slow walking speed and right pocket position using Sigmoid activation

reported_limitations: Poorest performance observed for bag positions due to instability and swinging

reproducibility.benchmark_used: Pervasive, UCI HAPT, CNU, OU ISIR

key_contribution: First exploratory study investigating elapsed time (1 month) for IMU gait authentication
Proposed A1TIL framework utilizing 1D-LTP and Kernelized Domain Adaptation (KDA)
Introduced MMUISD dataset focusing on time-invariant inertial gait signals
main_strength: Effective use of domain adaptation to mitigate performance degradation caused by temporal shifts
main_weakness: Lack of public availability of the proposed dataset at the time of publication
insights.notes_for_review: The study highlights that sensor position (pocket) and walking speed (slow) significantly influence EER, and that multimodal IMU data (acc + gyro) is superior to unimodal.
